answer:
**Old English Elegies** (particularly "Maxims II") contains explicit reflections on heroic qualities through a father's wisdom-teaching to his son. A steadfast hero (*fæstne æþele*) must be "wary with his words, thinking in his heart, not loud with clamor"; prudent and moderate, "wise in his mind, shrewd in his thoughts, and eager for wisdom"; guarding against anger and sin; loving and open-hearted.[^1] Elsewhere, heroism is tied to exceptional battle prowess as a youth, such as Offa marking borders "with his single sword," achieving "greater heroism in battle."[^2]

**Paraśara Smṛti** reflects on heroism (*vīra*) through the rewards of dying in battle: a hero obtains "imperishable worlds, provided he does not speak like a coward"; celestial maidens carry off the hero "whose body is cut and wounded by arrows, clubs, and staffs"; nymphs claim slain heroes as husbands; such heroes attain heavenly rewards "in a moment by giving up their lives in a good fight," equaling lifetimes of Brāhmaṇa sacrifices.[^3]

**Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana** portrays heroism in internal and external battles: "heroes who perform austerities in battle"; those "heroic in generosity"; the "brave, wise, and excellent hero" who practices fearless protection (*abhayadāna*); and supreme heroic power as "pure, immeasurable *dharma*."[^4]

**San Hyan Tattvajnana** defines a heroic *buddhi-sattva* by intense "courage, heroism, and valor"; not turning from danger, sacrificing life out of love; remaining "tranquil and clear when practicing heroism and courage."[^5]

**San Hyan Kamahayanikan** reflects on "heroic generosity" (*pāramitā dāna*) in supreme giving (*mahātidāna*), tested by giving flesh, eyes, and heart without pain or attachment, conquering inner demons.[^6]

Cynewulf's signed poems (*Andreas*, *Elene*, *Juliana*) repeatedly invoke "hero" (*beorn*, *wīgend*) for saintly figures enduring trials with faith, courage, and loyalty to God (e.g., Andrew as "battle-bold hero," "doughty hero"), but these are narrative epithets praising Christian virtue amid heroic ordeals rather than direct analysis of heroic essence.[^7] Similar epic usages appear in *Manusmṛti* (e.g., heroic posture in penance; slaying a hero as grave sin) and scattered Indic works (*Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, etc.), but without sustained reflection.[^8] No other works offer comparable reflection.

[^1]: *Old English Elegies*, [ch.17](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/17-thus-frod-faeder-freobearn-laerde/translation/#p-ebe5d4), [ch.17](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/17-thus-frod-faeder-freobearn-laerde/translation/#p-bf88d6).
[^2]: *Old English Elegies*, [ch.14](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/14-widsith-matholade-wordhord-onleac/translation/#p-237982), [ch.14](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/14-widsith-matholade-wordhord-onleac/translation/#p-6bde05).
[^3]: *Parāśara Smṛti*, [ch.3](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-857cff), [ch.3](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-bad701), [ch.3](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-fa4037), [ch.3](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-20fc90).
[^4]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, [ch.1](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-979379), [ch.1](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-989404), [ch.1](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-bb23f1), [ch.1](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f57d92).
[^5]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, [ch.1](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-4c272a).
[^6]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [ch.1](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-1d8bac).
[^7]: *Andreas*, [ch.1](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-74e29a); similar in *Elene* [ch.1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1) and *Juliana* [ch.1](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-529b94).
[^8]: *Manusmṛti*, [ch.12](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-0803db).
